task: Fix the stale CLI skill so agents using Datalox through the wrapper/CLI generate product trajectory rows with the same snippet and quality rules as the MCP path.
summary: The CLI fallback guidance now matches the MCP trajectory product path: product rows use record_trajectory or datalox record-trajectory, include standalone exact before/after snippets, and must repair rows downgraded by the quality gate before export.
observations: user: Asked whether the other thread using the CLI meant the MCP path had been updated but the CLI skill had not, then asked to fix it.
agent: Read the maintain-datalox-pack skill, the CLI skill, and the linked CLI note, confirming the active CLI guidance still said to use record_turn_result/datalox record after grounded outcomes.
tool: Scanned active docs for record_trajectory, record-trajectory, record_turn_result, qualityDowngraded, and snippet guidance to identify stale surfaces.
agent: Patched the CLI skill workflow to prefer MCP record_trajectory, fall back to CLI record-trajectory, require exact before/after snippets, and treat qualityDowngraded as a repair signal.
agent: Patched the linked CLI note so adopted hosts receive the same product trajectory guidance and no longer learn legacy record_turn_result as the post-run capture path.

agent: Added regression assertions to adoption and wrapper tests so copied host skill/note surfaces must contain record-trajectory guidance and must not contain the old exact record_turn_result instruction.
tool: Focused adoption and wrapper surface tests passed, including the new copied-skill regression checks.

task.prompt: Fix the stale CLI skill so agents using Datalox through the wrapper/CLI generate product trajectory rows with the same snippet and quality rules as the MCP path.
task.environment: Datalox pack instruction surfaces, adoption tests, and wrapper-surface tests

context.error: The MCP path had been updated to record standalone debugging_trajectory.v1 rows with exact before/after snippets, but the CLI fallback skill still told agents to use legacy record_turn_result/datalox record after grounded outcomes. A CLI-hosted agent could therefore keep producing old summary-shaped events without code snippets.

relevant_files.before:
1. If MCP tools are available in the active session, call `resolve_loop` before acting.
2. Use `record_turn_result` after meaningful grounded outcomes.
3. Treat native Codex chat with MCP as guidance-only unless a Datalox wrapper sentinel is present.
4. Treat Claude Code native skills and MCP as model-chosen guidance unless `currentSession.activeWrapper` is `"claude"` and `currentSession.wrapperEnforced` is `true`.
5. Treat the Claude Stop hook as post-turn sidecar automation only; it can record, compile, and maintain after Claude responds, but it cannot force pre-turn `resolve_loop`.
6. For enforceable Codex `exec`, use `datalox codex --repo <repo> --task "<task>" --prompt "<prompt>"`.
7. For enforceable Claude prompt runs, use `datalox claude --repo <repo> --task "<task>" --prompt "<prompt>" -- --print "<prompt>"` or the installed Claude shim when status proves it is automatic.
8. For other CLI hosts, use `datalox wrap command --repo <repo> --task "<task>" --prompt "<prompt>" -- <host-command> __DATALOX_PROMPT__`.
9. If the host cannot accept prompt placeholders, use `datalox wrap prompt` and pass the returned prompt to the host manually.
10. When the host has no automatic post-turn hook or wrapper, use `datalox record` or `datalox promote` explicitly after repeated corrections.
relevant_files.after:
1. If MCP tools are available in the active session, call `resolve_loop` before acting.
2. For buyer-facing trajectory dataset capture after coding/debugging work, build a `debugging_trajectory.v1` row and call MCP tool `record_trajectory` when it is available.
3. If MCP is not available, write the row JSON and run `datalox record-trajectory --repo <repo> --trajectory-row <row.json> --json`.
4. In every trajectory row, `context.relevant_files[].before` and `after` must be exact minimal code snippets from the changed source, not prose, pointers, or "see file" summaries. Put explanation in `context.notes`, `trajectory.content`, or `final.explanation`.
5. If `record_trajectory` or `record-trajectory` returns `qualityDowngraded: true`, repair the row before treating it as `curation.quality: "use"` or exporting it as training-grade data.
6. Use `record_turn_result` or `datalox record` only for legacy/internal guidance maintenance when no explicit trajectory row/session event is being captured.
7. Treat native Codex chat with MCP as guidance-only unless a Datalox wrapper sentinel is present.
8. Treat Claude Code native skills and MCP as model-chosen guidance unless `currentSession.activeWrapper` is `"claude"` and `currentSession.wrapperEnforced` is `true`.
9. Treat the Claude Stop hook as post-turn sidecar automation only; it can record, compile, and maintain after Claude responds, but it cannot force pre-turn `resolve_loop`.
10. For enforceable Codex `exec`, use `datalox codex --repo <repo> --task "<task>" --prompt "<prompt>"`.
11. For enforceable Claude prompt runs, use `datalox claude --repo <repo> --task "<task>" --prompt "<prompt>" -- --print "<prompt>"` or the installed Claude shim when status proves it is automatic.
12. For other CLI hosts, use `datalox wrap command --repo <repo> --task "<task>" --prompt "<prompt>" -- <host-command> __DATALOX_PROMPT__`.
13. If the host cannot accept prompt placeholders, use `datalox wrap prompt` and pass the returned prompt to the host manually.
14. When the host has no automatic post-turn hook or wrapper and the work is only repeated internal guidance maintenance, use `datalox record` or `datalox promote` explicitly after repeated corrections.

relevant_files.before: If MCP is available in the active session, call `resolve_loop` before acting and `record_turn_result` after meaningful grounded outcomes. Prefer `datalox codex` for enforceable Codex `exec` flows and `datalox claude` for enforceable Claude prompt runs. Otherwise use `datalox wrap command` with `__DATALOX_PROMPT__` or the `DATALOX_PROMPT` environment variable.
relevant_files.after:
If MCP is available in the active session, call `resolve_loop` before acting. For buyer-facing trajectory dataset capture after coding/debugging work, prefer MCP tool `record_trajectory` with a complete `debugging_trajectory.v1` row.

If MCP is not available, write the row JSON and run `datalox record-trajectory --repo <repo> --trajectory-row <row.json> --json`. The row must stand alone: `context.relevant_files[].before` and `after` must contain exact minimal code snippets from the changed source, not prose summaries or repository-path pointers. Put explanation in notes, trajectory messages, or final explanation fields.

Use `record_turn_result` or `datalox record` only for legacy/internal guidance maintenance when no explicit trajectory row/session event is being captured. If `record_trajectory` or `record-trajectory` returns `qualityDowngraded: true`, repair the row before treating it as `curation.quality: "use"` or exporting it as training-grade data.

Prefer `datalox codex` for enforceable Codex `exec` flows and `datalox claude` for enforceable Claude prompt runs. Otherwise use `datalox wrap command` with `__DATALOX_PROMPT__` or the `DATALOX_PROMPT` environment variable.

notes: The MCP implementation had already been tightened; the stale path was the CLI skill and note copied into adopted repos.
The fix keeps legacy record_turn_result available for internal guidance maintenance but removes it as the product trajectory recommendation.
Regression tests now check the copied host surfaces, not only the source skill file.

trajectory.content: Asked whether the other thread using the CLI meant the MCP path had been updated but the CLI skill had not, then asked to fix it.
Read the maintain-datalox-pack skill, the CLI skill, and the linked CLI note, confirming the active CLI guidance still said to use record_turn_result/datalox record after grounded outcomes.
Scanned active docs for record_trajectory, record-trajectory, record_turn_result, qualityDowngraded, and snippet guidance to identify stale surfaces.
trajectory.command: rg -n "record_turn_result|datalox record|record-trajectory|record_trajectory|qualityDowngraded|context\.relevant_files|before/after" DATALOX.md AGENTS.md docs/trajectory-dataset-schema.md docs/trajectory-training-readiness.md src/core -g '*.{md,ts}'

trajectory.content: Patched the CLI skill workflow to prefer MCP record_trajectory, fall back to CLI record-trajectory, require exact before/after snippets, and treat qualityDowngraded as a repair signal.
Patched the linked CLI note so adopted hosts receive the same product trajectory guidance and no longer learn legacy record_turn_result as the post-run capture path.
Added regression assertions to adoption and wrapper tests so copied host skill/note surfaces must contain record-trajectory guidance and must not contain the old exact record_turn_result instruction.
Focused adoption and wrapper surface tests passed, including the new copied-skill regression checks.

final.fix_summary: The CLI fallback guidance now matches the MCP trajectory product path: product rows use record_trajectory or datalox record-trajectory, include standalone exact before/after snippets, and must repair rows downgraded by the quality gate before export.
final.explanation: This fixes the mismatch where CLI-hosted agents could keep following legacy record_turn_result guidance even though the MCP path and dataset schema had moved to explicit debugging_trajectory.v1 rows. The tests now guard the copied/adopted host surfaces, which is where a fresh CLI agent would read the guidance.

outcome.label: success
outcome.command: npx vitest run tests/adoptionScripts.test.ts tests/wrapperSurfaces.test.ts && git diff --check
outcome.evidence: Vitest reported 2 files and 40 tests passed; git diff --check passed.

curation.notes: No secrets or user-private code are included; snippets are from this pack's guidance and tests.
